spec_match.rationale: The concept now demonstrates orbit controls through pointer-lock movementX/Y deltas and explains the Android attached-pointer path.

interactive.rationale: Visitors can request and release lock, reset the camera, change sensitivity, invert Y, choose input mode, and inspect live movement readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page lede and controls match the feature-index framing around 3D viewer orbit controls and Android pointer-lock support.

edge_cases.rationale: The page exposes no-fine-pointer touch fallback, missing API handling, request rejection catch handling, and pointerlockerror reporting.

references.rationale: The concept page directly links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6739764319485952.

design_system.rationale: The inline CSS uses shared design variables, keeps button background overrides paired with color, and preserves the existing stage/readout language.

summary: orbit-controls now clears the prior major and moderate critique items: it has real pointer-lock controls, live orbit telemetry, a touch/no-pointer fallback path, visible request-failure handling, and the required ChromeStatus self-heal link.
